articles.full_text:
Rain alert across all 75 UP districts:Temperatures drop by 10°C as storms and showers bring relief from heatwave conditions
- Copy link
Weather conditions have changed across Uttar Pradesh during the Nautapa period. Bright sunshine was seen from Sunday morning, but humidity in the air has brought relief from the intense heat. Cloud cover has also continued across many parts of the state.
A rain alert has been issued for all 75 districts of Uttar Pradesh today. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has also warned of thunderstorms in 57 districts, with wind speeds expected to reach up to 60 kmph.
Temperatures drop by up to 10°C after three days of rain and storms
Following three consecutive days of thunderstorms and rainfall, temperatures across the state have fallen significantly, dropping by as much as 10°C and settling around 38°C.
Over the past 24 hours, Moradabad recorded the state's highest temperature at 38.5°C. Other key readings included-
Just three days earlier, on May 28, Banda had recorded a scorching 47.5°C.
Rain likely to continue until June 1
According to the weather department, light rainfall is likely across the state on June 1 as well. Cool winds are expected to continue, while cloudy skies will help maintain relief from the heat. However, weather conditions are forecast to change again from June 2.
Temperatures are expected to rise by 4°C to 5°C, bringing warmer conditions back to the state. Despite the increase, no heatwave conditions are expected at present.
See 5 pictures-
Moist winds from Sea regions behind weather shift
Lucknow-based meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh said-
Moist winds arriving from the Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea have altered weather conditions across Uttar Pradesh. Thunderstorms and rainfall have been occurring due to an active western disturbance since May 28. Its impact is expected to continue until June 1.
He added that western Uttar Pradesh and the Terai belt face the highest risk of storms. Weather conditions are expected to change again from June 2, leading to a rise in temperatures. However, heatwave conditions are not expected for now.
How will the weather be over the next five days?
June 1- Scattered showers are likely at several places in western Uttar Pradesh and at isolated locations in eastern Uttar Pradesh.
June 2- Weather is expected to remain clear across the state. The absence of heatwave conditions will continue to provide relief from extreme heat.
June 3- Skies will remain clear across Uttar Pradesh. Temperatures will rise and warmer conditions will return.
June 4- Clear skies are expected in both eastern and western Uttar Pradesh. Hot winds will blow, and the heat is likely to intensify once again.
June 5- Light rainfall is possible in western Uttar Pradesh, while eastern Uttar Pradesh is expected to remain dry. Hot weather conditions are likely to persist.
